```python
121	@dataclass
121	class CacheEntry:
122	    value: BaseGrammarObject
122	    event: Event
124	

125	class BaseGrammarBackend:
126	    def __init__(self):
126	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
126	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
128	

130	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
130	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
131	

133	    def dispatch_fallback(
133	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
133	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
138	        """
138	        This function should not be reached in any case.
138	        """
140	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
141	

143	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
143	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
145	

146	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
148	

150	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
150	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
151	

153	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
153	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
154	

156	    def _init_value_dispatch(
156	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
156	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
158	        s = time.perf_counter()
158	        key_type, key_string = key
159	        if key_type == "json":
160	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
161	        elif key_type == "regex":
161	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
162	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
162	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
163	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
168	        else:
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
170	

171	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
171	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
171	        return grammar
172	

174	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
174	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
174	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
176	        value = self.cache.get(key)
176	        if value:
176	            copied_value = value.copy()
176	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
176	            return copied_value, True
180	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
180	        return value, False
181	

183	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
183	        self.cache[key] = value
184	

186	    def reset(self):
186	        self.cache.clear()
187	

189	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
190	

192	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
192	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
193	

195	def create_grammar_backend(
195	    server_args: ServerArgs,
195	    tokenizer,
195	    vocab_size: int,
195	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
199	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
199	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
200	

202	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
202	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
202	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
204	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
204	        )
205	

207	    # Default grammar backends
207	    if name == "outlines":
207	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
220	

221	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
221	            tokenizer,
221	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
221	        )
224	    elif name == "xgrammar":
224	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
224	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
224	        )
228	

229	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
229	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
230	

231	        try:
231	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
231	                tokenizer,
231	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
231	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
231	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
231	        )
237	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
237	            logger.warning(
237	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
237	                f"Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
237	                f"Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
237	        )
243	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
243	            return None
244	    elif name == "llguidance":
244	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
252	

253	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
253	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
253	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
253	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
253	        )
257	    elif name == "none":
257	        return None
258	    else:
259	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
260	

261	        if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
261	            from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
261	                ReasonerGrammarBackend,
261	            )
265	

266	            grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
266	                grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
266	            )
268	

269	    return grammar_backend
```

143	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
144	                )
145	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
146	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
147	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
148	                    raise ValueError(
149	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
150	                        f"{token_id}."
151	                    )
152	
153	        grammars = [
154	            self.json_schema,
155	            self.regex,
155	            self.ebnf,
156	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
157	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
158	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
159	
160	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
161	        # Process stop strings
162	        if self.stop_strs is None:
163	            self.stop_strs = []
164	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
164	        else:
164	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
164	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
164	
165	            stop_str_max_len = 0
172	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
173	                if tokenizer is not None:
174	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
175	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
176	                else:
177	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
178	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
179	
180	        # Process stop regex strings
181	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
182	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
182	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
183	        else:
183	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
183	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
183	
184	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
189	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
190	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
191	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
192	                )
193	
194	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
195	
196	
197	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
198	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
199	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
200	# token
201	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
202	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
202	
203	
204	MAX_LEN = 2**30
205	
206	
207	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
208	    total = 0
208	    for token, value in subpattern:
209	        if token in {
209	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
210	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
210	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
210	        }:
210	            total += 1
210	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
217	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
217	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
217	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
217	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
217	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
222	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
224	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
224	            _, branches = value
224	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
226	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
227	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
228	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
230	                total += MAX_LEN
231	            else:
231	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
233	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
233	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
233	            # length
233	            total += 0
236	        else:
237	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
238	
238	            total += MAX_LEN
240	
241	    return total
242	```

```python
125	class BaseGrammarBackend:
126	    def __init__(self):
126	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
126	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
128	

130	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
130	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
131	

133	    def dispatch_fallback(
133	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
133	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
138	        """
138	        This function should not be reached in any case.
138	        """
140	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
141	

143	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
143	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
145	

146	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
148	

150	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
150	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
151	

153	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
153	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
154	

156	    def _init_value_dispatch(
156	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
156	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
158	        s = time.perf_counter()
158	        key_type, key_string = key
159	        if key_type == "json":
160	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
161	        elif key_type == "regex":
161	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
162	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
162	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
163	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
168	        else:
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
170	

171	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
171	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
171	        return grammar
172	

174	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
174	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
174	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
176	        value = self.cache.get(key)
176	        if value:
176	            copied_value = value.copy()
176	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
176	            return copied_value, True
180	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
180	        return value, False
181	

183	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
183	        self.cache[key] = value
184	

186	    def reset(self):
186	        self.cache.clear()
187	

189	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

192	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
192	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
193	

195	def create_grammar_backend(
195	    server_args: ServerArgs,
195	    tokenizer,
195	    vocab_size: int,
195	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
199	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
199	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
200	

202	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
202	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
202	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
204	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
204	        )
205	

207	    # Default grammar backends
207	    if name == "outlines":
207	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
220	

221	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
221	            tokenizer,
221	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
221	        )
224	    elif name == "xgrammar":
224	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
224	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
224	        )
228	

229	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
229	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
230	

231	        try:
231	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
231	                tokenizer,
231	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
231	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
231	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
231	        )
237	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
237	            logger.warning(
237	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
237	                f"Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
237	                f"Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
237	        )
243	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
243	            return None
244	    elif name == "llguidance":
244	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
252	

253	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
253	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
253	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
253	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
253	        )
257	    elif name == "none":
257	        return None
258	    else:
259	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
260	

261	        if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
261	            from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
261	                ReasonerGrammarBackend,
261	            )
265	

266	            grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
266	                grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
266	            )
268	

269	    return grammar_backend
```

```
   174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)

   176	        except Exception as e:
```

```
   114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
   115	    def __init__(
   116	        self,

   130	
   131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
   132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
   133	            )
   134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
   135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
   136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
   137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
   138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
   139	            )
   140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
   141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
   142	            )
   143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
   144	
   145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
   146	        try:
   147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
   147	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
   148	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
   149	            else:

   152	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:

   154	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
   155	
   156	        jump_forward_map = None
   157	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
   158

   170	            )
   171	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
   172	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
   173	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
   174	        return self._compile_regex(regex)

   180	def build_regex_from_object(

   182	):

   185	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
   186	        schema = json.dumps(object)
   187	    else:
```

## Key Design Decision: How to implement `json_path` constraint

The core challenge is: how do we constrain generation to produce valid JSON that satisfies a JSONPath expression?

JSONPath like `$.store.book[*].author` means the output must be JSON where:
- There's a root object
- With a key "store" containing an object
- With a key "book" containing an array
- Where each array element has an "author" key

The approach I'll use is: **Convert the JSONPath expression to a JSON Schema, then dispatch via the existing `json` dispatch mechanism.**


This is elegant because all backends already support JSON schema, so I can avoid adding new backend-specific code. The conversion from JSONPath to JSON schema is a well-defined translation that I can implement in the grammar manager layer. I'll work through the conversion algorithm by mapping JSONPath expressions to their corresponding schema requirements—for instance, `$.foo` becomes a schema requiring a root object with that key, while `$.store.book[*].author` requires nested objects and arrays with specific properties.

For the initial implementation, I'll focus on the most common JSONPath patterns: root access, object property access with dot or bracket notation, array index access, wildcard array elements, and I'll defer recursive descent for now. When converting a path like `$.store.book[*].author`, I'll parse it into segments and build up a schema that enforces the required structure...

Generating the schema structure... I'm laying out the implementation plan for integrating JSONPath constraints into the system. I need to create a new module to convert JSONPath expressions into JSON schemas, update the sampling parameters to accept a JSONPath option, and then integrate this into the grammar manager to handle the constraint validation. for `json_path` in `process_req_with_grammar()` - I'll convert the JSONPath to a JSON schema before dispatching it as `("json", derived_schema)`, reusing the existing json dispatch without needing a new type.

I need to add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` to `SglSamplingParams` in the IR module, then expose it through the `gen()` function in the API layer and pass it along to `SglGen`. In the OpenAI protocol handler, I'll add the `json_path` field to request models and include it in `to_sampling_params()` and the constraint checking logic. Then I'll wire it through the completions serving layer and create a test file with simple and nested JSONPath examples.

Actually, looking at how `grammar_manager.py` handles constraints - it checks for `json_schema`, `regex`, and `ebnf` in sequence to determine the dispatch key. I should follow that same pattern for `json_path`. I'm deciding between two approaches for handling JSONPath caching. Using a `json_path` dispatch handler with the original path string as the cache key keeps things clean and user-friendly, so I'll go with adding a `dispatch_json_path()` method to the base backend that converts to schema internally and delegates to the existing JSON handler—this avoids duplicating logic across backends.

Now I'm thinking through the JSONPath parser implementation...

Writing parser logic... Writing the parser logic... Continuing to parse the bracket notation by extracting integer indices with optional negative signs, then validating the closing bracket before adding the segment. Now I'm building the schema from these parsed segments, starting with an empty schema for a bare dollar sign.

Building the schema backwards through the segments, where each property wraps the previous schema in an object structure, wildcards create array wrappers, and indices handle both positive and negative cases to determine the required array depth. I'm verifying the logic works for simpler paths too—`$.name` correctly produces an object with a name property, and tracing through the more complex `$.store.book[*].author` example shows the schema builds up properly from the innermost property outward through the array and parent objects. I'm working through how the schema builds up step by step as I traverse the JSONPath segments in reverse order, starting from the innermost property and wrapping it with array and object constraints as I move outward. Now I need to organize this logic into a separate module for the JSONPath parsing to keep things modular and testable.

I'm mapping out all the files that need changes across the sampling parameters, grammar management, language IR, and API layers to wire up the new JSONPath constraint support end-to-end. Looking at the `SglSamplingParams` dataclass, I see it only has `json_schema` and `regex` for constraints—no `ebnf` or `structural_tag`—which means the frontend API is simpler than the backend. For the OpenAI-compatible API layer, the request models apparently support both `regex` and `ebnf` based on the `to_sampling_params` conversion method, so I'll need to check how those map to the sampling parameters and where `json_path` should fit in.

Now I'm weighing two approaches for handling the JSONPath conversion: either doing it directly in the grammar manager when processing requests, which keeps things simple but makes the cache key verbose, or adding a dedicated dispatch method in the base backend that converts JSONPath to JSON schema before delegating to the existing JSON handler, which is cleaner and preserves the original path as the cache key.

I'm leaning toward Option B since it fits the existing dispatch pattern better. I need to understand where JSONPath fits into this flow and whether I should add it as another condition in that chain or handle it differently.

The approach would be to add json_path to both the condition check and the key assignment in grammar_manager.py, then implement a dispatch_json_path method in the base backend that converts the JSONPath to a schema and delegates to the existing json dispatch logic. 141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}

147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)

186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:

223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,

250	        if stop is None:

257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:

320	        if tracing_scope is None:

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):
620	    def __init__(self, model_type: str, expr: SglExpr):
621	        super().__init__()
622	        self.model_type = model_type
623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

26	class RuntimeEndpoint(BaseBackend):
27	    def __init__(
28	        self,
29	        base_url: str,
30	        api_key: Optional[str] = None,
31	        verify: Optional[str] = None,
32	        chat_template_name: Optional[str] = None,
33	    ):
34	        super().__init__()
35	        self.support_concate_and_append = True
36	
37	        self.base_url = base_url
38	        self.api_key = api_key
39	        self.verify = verify
40	
41	        res = http_request(
42	            self.base_url + "/get_model_info",
43	            api_key=self.api_key,
44	            verify=self.verify,
45	        )
46	        self._assert_success(res)
47	        self.model_info = res.json()
48	
49	        if chat_template_name:
50	            self.chat_template = get_chat_template(chat_template_name)
51	        else:
52	            self.chat_template = get_chat_template_by_model_path(
53	                self.model_info["model_path"]
54	            )
55	
56	    def get_model_name(self):

65	        )
66	        self._assert_success(res)
67

76	
77	    def get_chat_template(self):

84	            api_key=self.api_key,

86	        )

89	    def start_profile(self):

116	    def fill_image(self, s: StreamExecutor):
117	        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
118	        self._add_images(s, data)
119	        res = http_request(

121	            json=data,

126	
127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158	
159	    def generate(
160	        self,
161	        s: StreamExecutor,
162	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
163	    ):
164	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
165	        data = {
166	            "text": s.text_,
167	            "sampling_params": {
168	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
169	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
170	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
171	            },
172	        }
173	
174	        for item in [
175	            "return_logprob",
176	            "logprob_start_len",
177	            "top_logprobs_num",
178	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
179	        ]:
180	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
181	            if value is not None:
182	                data[item] = value
183	
184	        self._add_images(s, data)
185	
186	        res = http_request(
187	            self.base_url + "/generate",
188	            json=data,
189	            api_key=self.api_key,
190	            verify=self.verify,
191	        )
192	        self._assert_success(res)
193	
194	        obj = res.json()
195	        comp = obj["text"]
196	        return comp, obj["meta_info"]
197	
198	    def generate_stream(
199	        self,
200	        s: StreamExecutor,
201	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
202	    ):
203	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
204	
205	        data = {
206	            "text": s.text_,
207	            "sampling_params": {
208	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
209	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
210	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
211	            },
212	        }
213	
214	        for item in [
215	            "return_logprob",
216	            "logprob_start_len",
217	            "top_logprobs_num",
218	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
219	        ]:
220	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
221	            if value is not None:
222	                data[item] = value
223	
224	        data["stream"] = True
225	        self._add_images(s, data)
226	
227	        res = http_request(
228	            self.base_url + "/generate",
229	            json=data,
230	            stream=True,
231	            api_key=self.api_key,
232	            verify=self.verify,
233	        )
234	        self._assert_success(res)
235	        pos = 0
236	
237	        for chunk in res.iter_lines(decode_unicode=False):
238	            chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
239	            if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
240	                if chunk == "data: [DONE]":
241	                    break
242	                data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
243	                chunk_text = data["text"][pos:]
244	                meta_info = data["meta_info"]
245	                pos += len(chunk_text)
246	                yield chunk_text, meta_info
247	
248	    def select(
249	        self,
250	        s: StreamExecutor,
251	        choices: List[str],
252	        temperature: float,
253	        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
254	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
255	        assert temperature <= 1e-5
256	
257	        # Cache common prefix
258	        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
259	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
260	        prompt_len = obj["meta_info"]["prompt_tokens"]
261	        logprob_start_len = max(prompt_len - 2, 0)  # For token healing
262	
263	        # Compute logprob
264	        data = {
265	            "text": [s.text_ + c for c in choices],
266	            "sampling_params": {
267	                "max_new_tokens": 0,
268	                "temperature": 0,
269	            },
270	            "return_logprob": True,
271	            "return_text_in_logprobs": True,
272	            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
273	        }
274	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
275	
276	        input_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
277	        output_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["output_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
278	        normalized_prompt_logprobs = [
279	            compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"])
280	            for r in obj
281	        ]
282	
283	        # Remove extra token if no token healing occurred
284	        for i in range(len(input_token_logprobs)):
285	            healed_token_str = input_token_logprobs[i][0][-1]
286	            if s.text_.endswith(healed_token_str):
287	                healed_token_logprob = input_token_logprobs[i][0][0]
288	                normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] = (
289	                    normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] * len(input_token_logprobs[i])
290	                    - healed_token_logprob
291	                ) / (len(input_token_logprobs[i]) - 1)
292	                input_token_logprobs[i] = input_token_logprobs[i][1:]
293	
294	        # Compute unconditional logprobs if required
295	        if choices_method.requires_unconditional_logprobs:
296	            input_ids = [[el[1] for el in subl] for subl in input_token_logprobs]
297	            data = {
298	                "input_ids": input_ids,
299	                "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0},
300	                "return_logprob": True,
301	            }
302	            obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
303	            unconditional_token_logprobs = [
304	                r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj
305	            ]
306	        else:
307	            unconditional_token_logprobs = None
308	
309	        return choices_method(
310	            choices=choices,
311	            normalized_prompt_logprobs=normalized_prompt_logprobs,
312	            input_token_logprobs=input_token_logprobs,
313	            output_token_logprobs=output_token_logprobs,
314	            unconditional_token_logprobs=unconditional_token_logprobs,
315	        )
316	
317	    def concatenate_and_append(self, src_rids: List[str], dst_rid: str):
318	        res = http_request(
319	            self.base_url + "/concate_and_append_request",
320	            json={"src_rids": src_rids, "dst_rid": dst_rid},
321	            api_key=self.api_key,
322	            verify=self.verify,
323	        )
324	        self._assert_success(res)
325	
326	    def _generate_http_request(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
327	        self._add_images(s, data)
328	        res = http_request(
329	            self.base_url + "/generate",
330	            json=data,
331	            api_key=self.api_key,
332	            verify=self.verify,
333	        )
334	        self._assert_success(res)
335	        return res.json()
336	
337	    def _add_images(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
338	        if s.images_:
339	            assert len(s.images_) == 1, "Only support one image."
340	            data["image_data"] = s.images_[0][1]
341	
342	    def _assert_success(self, res):
343	        if res.status_code != 200:
344	            try:
345	                content = res.json()
346	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
347	                content = res.text
348	            raise RuntimeError(content)
349	
350	
351	def compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(input_logprobs):
352	    values = [x[0] for x in input_logprobs if x[0]]
353	    return sum(values) / len(values)

356	class Runtime:
357	    """
358	    A wrapper for the HTTP server.
359	    This is used for launching the server in a python program without
360	    using the command line interface.
361	
362	    It is mainly used for the frontend language.
363	    You should use the Engine class if you want to do normal offline processing without the frontend language.
364	    """
365	
366	    def __init__(
367	        self,
368	        log_level: str = "error",
369	        launch_timeout: float = 300.0,
370	        *args,
371	        **kwargs,
372	    ):
373	        """See the arguments in server_args.py::ServerArgs
374	
375	        Args:
376	            log_level: Log level for the server.
377	            timeout: Timeout in seconds for waiting for the server to start.
378	            *args: Additional arguments passed to ServerArgs.
379	            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to ServerArgs.
380	        """
381	        # We delay the import of any `sglang.srt` components in `sglang.lang`, so users can run
382	        # client code without installing SRT server and its dependency if they want.
383	        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.http_server import launch_server
384	        from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
385	        from sglang.srt.utils import is_port_available
386	
387	        self.server_args = ServerArgs(*args, log_level=log_level, **kwargs)
388	
389	        # Pre-allocate ports
390	        for port in range(self.server_args.port, 40000):
391	            if is_port_available(port):
392	                break
393	        self.server_args.port = port
394	
395	        self.url = self.server_args.url()
396	        self.generate_url = self.url + "/generate"
397	
398	        # NOTE: We store pid instead of proc to fix some issues during __delete__
399	        self.pid = None
400	
401	        ctx = multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")
402	        proc = ctx.Process(
403	            target=launch_server,
404	            args=(self.server_args,),
405	        )
406	        proc.start()
407	        self.pid = proc.pid
408	
409	        # Before python program terminates, call shutdown implicitly. Therefore, users don't have to explicitly call .shutdown()
410	        atexit.register(self.shutdown)
411	
412	        # Wait for server to be ready by polling /health_generate
413	        start_time = time.time()
414	        with requests.Session() as session:
415	            while time.time() - start_time < launch_timeout:
416	                try:
417	                    response = session.get(f"{self.url}/health_generate")
418	                    if response.status_code == 200:
419	                        break
420	                except requests.RequestException:
421	                    pass
422	
423	                if not proc.is_alive():
424	                    self.shutdown()
425	                    raise RuntimeError(
426	                        "Initialization failed. Please see the error messages above."
427	                    )
428	
429	                time.sleep(2)
430	            else:
431	                self.shutdown()
432	                raise TimeoutError("Server failed to start within the timeout period.")
433	
434	        self.endpoint = RuntimeEndpoint(self.url)
435	
436	    def shutdown(self):
437	        from sglang.srt.utils import kill_process_tree
438	
439	        if self.pid is not None:
440	            kill_process_tree(self.pid)
441	            self.pid = None
442	
443	    def start_profile(self):
444	        self.endpoint.start_profile()
445	
446	    def stop_profile(self):
447	        self.endpoint.stop_profile()
448	
449	    def cache_prefix(self, prefix: str):
450	        self.endpoint.cache_prefix(prefix)
451	
452	    def get_tokenizer(self):
453	        from sglang.srt.utils.hf_transformers_utils import get_tokenizer
454	
455	        return get_tokenizer(
456	            self.server_args.tokenizer_path,
457	            tokenizer_mode=self.server_args.tokenizer_mode,
458	            trust_remote_code=self.server_args.trust_remote_code,
459	            revision=self.server_args.revision,
460	        )
461	
462	    async def async_generate(
463	        self,
464	        prompt: str,
465	        sampling_params: Optional[Dict] = None,
466	    ):
467	        if self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
468	            json_data = {
469	                "input_ids": prompt,
470	                "sampling_params": sampling_params,
471	                "stream": True,
472	            }
473	        else:
474	            json_data = {
475	                "text": prompt,
476	                "sampling_params": sampling_params,
477	                "stream": True,
478	            }
479	        pos = 0
480	
481	        timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=3 * 3600)
482	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession(timeout=timeout, trust_env=True) as session:
483	            async with session.post(self.generate_url, json=json_data) as response:
484	                async for chunk, _ in response.content.iter_chunks():
485	                    chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
486	                    if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
487	                        if chunk == "data: [DONE]\n\n":
488	                            break
489	                        data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
490	                        if "text" in data:
491	                            cur = data["text"][pos:]
492	                            if cur:
493	                                yield cur
494	                            pos += len(cur)
495	                        else:
496	                            yield data
497	
498	    add_request = async_generate
499	
500	    def generate(
501	        self,
502	        prompt: Union[str, List[str]],
503	        sampling_params: Optional[Dict] = None,
504	        return_logprob: Optional[Union[List[bool], bool]] = False,
505	        logprob_start_len: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None,
506	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None,
507	        lora_path: Optional[List[Optional[str]]] = None,
508	    ):
509	        json_data = {
510	            "text": prompt,
511	            "sampling_params": sampling_params,
512	            "return_logprob": return_logprob,
513	            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
514	            "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
515	            "lora_path": lora_path,
516	        }
517	        assert not isinstance(lora_path, list) or len(lora_path) == len(prompt)
518	        response = requests.post(
519	            self.url + "/generate",
520	            json=json_data,
521	        )
522	        return json.dumps(response.json())
523	
524	    def encode(
525	        self,
526	        prompt: Union[str, List[str], List[Dict], List[List[Dict]]],
527	    ):
528	        json_data = {"text": prompt}
529	        response = requests.post(self.url + "/encode", json=json_data)
530	        return json.dumps(response.json())
531	
532	    async def get_server_info(self):
533	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
534	            async with session.get(f"{self.url}/get_server_info") as response:
535	                if response.status == 200:
536	                    return await response.json()
537	                else:
538	                    error_data = await response.json()
539	                    raise RuntimeError(
540	                        f"Failed to get server info. {error_data['error']['message']}"
541	                    )
542	
543	    def __del__(self):
544	        self.shutdown()

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""

    if choices:
        return SglSelect(
            name,
            choices,
            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
        )

    # check regex is valid
    if regex is not None:
        try:
            re.compile(regex)
        except re.error as e:
            raise e

    return SglGen(
        name,
        max_tokens,
        min_tokens,
        n,
        stop,
        stop_token_ids,
        stop_regex,
        temperature,
        top_p,
        top_k,
        min_p,
        frequency_penalty,
        presence_penalty,
        ignore_eos,
        return_logprob,
        logprob_start_len,
        top_logprobs_num,
        return_text_in_logprobs,
        dtype,
        regex,
        json_schema,
    )
```

---

## **FLOW SUMMARY: How json_schema flows through the system**

1. **Frontend API** (`api.py`): User calls `gen(json_schema="...")` 
2. **IR Layer** (`ir.py`): Creates `SglGen(json_schema=...)` which stores in `SglSamplingParams(json_schema=...)`
3. **Backend Runtime** (`runtime_endpoint.py`): Calls `sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs()` which includes `json_schema` in the dict
4. **HTTP Transport**: JSON payload sent to `/generate` endpoint with `json_schema` field
5. **Server Protocol** (`protocol.py`): `CompletionRequest` accepts `json_schema` directly; `ChatCompletionRequest` extracts it from `response_format` validator

## Key Design Decision: JSONPath → JSON Schema Conversion

The `json_path` constraint will:
1. Accept a JSONPath expression like `$.store.book[*].author`
2. Convert it to a JSON Schema that enforces the required structure
3. Dispatch through the existing `json` backend infrastructure

## Implementation Strategy

The cleanest approach is:
1. Create a new module `json_path.py` with the conversion logic
2. Add `json_path` as a new field throughout the stack (SamplingParams, SglSamplingParams, API, protocol)


3. In `grammar_manager.py`, convert `json_path` → `("json_path", path_str)` key
4. In `base_grammar_backend.py`, add `dispatch_json_path()` which converts to schema and calls `dispatch_json()`
5. All existing backends automatically inherit `json_path` support

## What needs to change

I need to create a new `json_path.py` module with the conversion function, then add the `json_path` parameter to `sampling_params.py` along with verification logic. I'll also need to update the other files in the stack to thread this parameter through. Looking at the `SglGen` class structure, I need to add `json_path` to the constructor and pass it through to `SglSamplingParams`, then decide whether to include it in `clone()` and `to_srt_kwargs()`. Based on how `regex` and `json_schema` are handled—where they appear in `to_srt_kwargs()` but `regex` is excluded from `clone()`—I should follow the same pattern for `json_path` since it's a constrained generation parameter. I'm realizing that `clone()` intentionally excludes constraint-specific fields like `regex`, so I should follow that same pattern and not include `json_path` in `clone()` either—this keeps the constraints separate from the core sampling parameters that get cloned during request forking or caching.

Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a `json_path` output constraint to SGLang.

## Goal
Add a new constraint type called `json_path` that:
1. Accepts a JSONPath expression (e.g., `$.store.book[*].author`)
2. Converts it to a JSON Schema that enforces the required structure
3. Dispatches through the existing `json` backend infrastructure (reuses all backends: xgrammar, outlines, llguidance)
4. Plugs into the same constraint pipeline as `regex`, `json_schema`, `ebnf`

## Key Design Decision
**Convert JSONPath → JSON Schema, then reuse existing `dispatch_json()` infrastructure.**

The `dispatch_json_path()` method on `BaseGrammarBackend` will:
1. Parse the JSONPath expression into a JSON Schema
2. Call `self.dispatch_json(derived_schema)` 
3. All backends automatically inherit support with zero backend-specific code

## JSONPath → JSON Schema Conversion Logic
Parse the path and build schema from inside-out:

- `$.name` → `{"type":"object","properties":{"name":{}},"required":["name"]}`
- `$.store.book[*].author` → nested object → array (items) → object with "author" required
- `$.items[0].name` → object with "items" array with `minItems:1`, items have "name" 
- `$` alone → `{}` (accepts any valid JSON)

Supported segments: `.key` dot notation, `['key']`/`["key"]` bracket notation, `[n]` integer index, `[*]` wildcard. Recursive descent `..` should raise a clear error.

41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,

44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,

52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,

72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total
244

1	from __future__ import annotations

3	import logging
4	import time
5	from concurrent import futures
6	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List
7	
8	import torch
9	
10	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
11	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
12	    create_grammar_backend,
13	)
14	from sglang.srt.environ import envs
15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
196

42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None

49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:

54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()

113	    ) -> None:

116	        """

123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197	
198	
199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend
275

68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,

73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
